name: Cipaille or Cipate (Layered Meat Pie)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 tsp Mixed ground cloves, nutmeg, cinnamon, allspice
recipeInstructions:
Traditionally this layered pie is best made with game. If game meat is unavailable, use a mix of meats as in this recipe.
Servings: 8 to 10
1. Cut chicken, beef, and pork into 1-inch cubes and place in a large bowl. Combine with onions; cover and refrigerate for at least 12 hours or overnight.
2. Arrange salt pork evenly in the bottom of a 3-quart casserole, preferably cast iron with a lid. Layer with about one-third of the meat mixture and one-third of the cubed potatoes. Season with about one-third of the salt, pepper, and spices.
3. Roll out half of the pastry slightly thicker than usual and arrange on the potato layer, cutting a small hole in the center. Repeat with two more layers of meat and potatoes, seasoning again with salt, pepper, and spices.
4. Cover with remaining pastry, cutting a small hole in the center.
5. Slowly add enough chicken stock through the hole until liquid appears.
6. Cover dish and bake in a preheated 400°F oven for 45 minutes or until liquid simmers.
7. Reduce temperature to 250°F and continue to bake, covered, for 5 to 6 hours more or until top crust is a rich golden brown.
